name: Drug Interaction Patient
description: Patient with a serious drug-drug interaction: Warfarin + Fluconazole (Warfarin potentiation — risk of bleeding). System must detect, flag for review, and require clinician acknowledgment. Summary can be generated with review flag.

medications_discharge.name: Fluconazole
medications_discharge.interaction_note: SERIOUS INTERACTION: Fluconazole inhibits CYP2C9, significantly potentiating Warfarin effect. INR monitoring required.

drug_interactions.mechanism: Fluconazole inhibits CYP2C9, the primary metabolic enzyme for Warfarin (S-enantiomer). This significantly increases Warfarin plasma concentration and anticoagulation effect.
drug_interactions.clinical_consequence: Risk of supratherapeutic INR and major bleeding events
drug_interactions.management: Reduce Warfarin dose by 25-50%, monitor INR 2-3 times weekly during Fluconazole course

conflicts.description: Warfarin + Fluconazole: Serious CYP2C9-mediated interaction. Fluconazole will significantly potentiate Warfarin. INR monitoring plan required.

clinical_context.hospital_course: 80-year-old male admitted with oral candidiasis causing poor oral intake. Evaluated by nutrition. Warfarin continued for AFib. Fluconazole prescribed for candida treatment.
follow_up: PCP in 3 days for INR check
Anticoagulation clinic for INR monitoring during Fluconazole course

test_purpose: Validates drug-drug interaction detection. Unlike the allergy conflict (SCN-005 which BLOCKS), this interaction is serious but manageable — system should allow summary generation with clinician review flags and management guidance.
